data.name: AppTagline
data.value: Find, repair, and roll back Windows problems safely.

data.name: Hero_Title
data.value: Find the cause. Fix it safely.

data.name: Hero_Description
data.value: CaYaFix measures the system first and recommends only relevant repairs. Every persistent change is backed up and verified afterward.

data.name: Dashboard_ModulesHint
data.value: If you know the symptom, scan the relevant area directly.

data.name: Dashboard_SafetyText
data.value: CaYaFix prepares a System Restore point and action-level backups. A repair does not start if its required backup fails.

data.name: Findings_Description
data.value: Safe repairs are selected by default. Expand technical details to inspect the evidence behind each finding.

data.name: GuidedRepair_Intro
data.value: If something still feels wrong, pick a symptom below. CaYaFix can run the related Safe/Moderate repairs even when diagnostics stayed green.

data.name: GuidedRepair_IntroRisk
data.value: These repairs change system settings and can temporarily disturb things that currently work. Read the side-effects list before you continue.

data.name: GuidedRepair_PickHint
data.value: Choose a symptom on Findings, then confirm the risk warning to apply related repairs.

data.name: GuidedRepair_Warning
data.value: You are about to apply several related repairs without a failed diagnostic for each step. This can change working settings, restart services or devices, and may leave the system briefly unstable. Only continue if you understand the risk and have a way to undo (Recovery / System Restore).

data.name: GuidedRepair_WarningShort
data.value: Guided symptom repair: changes may affect working settings.

data.name: GuidedRepair_SideEffectsTitle
data.value: What you may notice while this runs

data.name: GuidedRepair_AcceptRisk
data.value: I understand these changes can break something that currently works, and I accept the risk.

data.name: GuidedRepair_NeedConsent
data.value: Accept the risk checkbox before applying symptom repairs.

data.name: GuidedRepair_NoFixes
data.value: No automatic Safe/Moderate repairs are mapped for this symptom.

data.name: GuidedRepair_RunningEffectsReminder
data.value: Side effects may appear now (audio glitches, brief disconnects, flicker). Stay on this window until finished.

data.name: GuidedRepair_Effects_Network
data.value: Internet may drop for a short time, Wiâ€‘Fi/Ethernet can reconnect, VPN sessions may end, downloads can pause, and sites may fail until DNS/cache settles.

data.name: GuidedRepair_Effects_Audio
data.value: You may hear crackling, brief silence, device switches, volume jumps, or Bluetooth headsets reconnecting. Close critical calls if possible.

data.name: GuidedRepair_Effects_Display
data.value: The screen may flicker, go black briefly, change resolution/refresh rate, or re-detect monitors.

data.name: GuidedRepair_Effects_Bluetooth
data.value: Paired devices may disconnect and need to reconnect; audio/input can drop for several seconds.

data.name: GuidedRepair_Effects_Printer
data.value: Print jobs may be cancelled or restarted; the printer can go offline briefly while the spooler restarts.

data.name: GuidedRepair_Effects_Disk
data.value: Disk activity can spike, the PC may feel slower, and a scheduled check may require a restart later.

data.name: GuidedRepair_Effects_Integrity
data.value: System file repair can take a long time, use CPU/disk heavily, and may ask for a reboot. Do not interrupt power.

data.name: GuidedRepair_Effects_Update
data.value: Windows Update services restart; pending installs may pause, and a reboot can be required afterward.

data.name: GuidedRepair_Effects_Store
data.value: Microsoft Store and some apps may close or re-sign-in; downloads can reset.

data.name: GuidedRepair_Effects_Camera
data.value: Camera apps may lose the device briefly; privacy prompts can reappear.

data.name: GuidedRepair_Effects_Usb
data.value: USB devices can disconnect and reappear; storage drives should be closed first to avoid interrupted transfers.

data.name: GuidedRepair_Effects_Search
data.value: Search may stop responding while the service restarts; indexing can resume with higher disk use.

data.name: GuidedRepair_Effects_Time
data.value: The clock may jump; secure sites or tokens can fail until time sync finishes.

data.name: GuidedRepair_Effects_Performance
data.value: Startup items or power plans may change; the PC can feel different after the next reboot.

data.name: GuidedRepair_Effects_Generic
data.value: Services or devices in this area may restart. Expect brief freezes, reconnects, or UI flicker while changes apply.

data.name: Operation_ResultHealthyAdviceGuided
data.value: If something still feels wrong, open Findings and pick a symptom â€” CaYaFix can apply related repairs after a risk warning (audio glitches, network drops, or flicker may occur while it runs).

data.name: Finding_NoAutomaticFix
data.value: No automatic repair is offered; review the evidence and the suggested manual checks.

data.name: Handoff_Description
data.value: The selected safe repair paths did not clear every finding. CaYaFix will not repeat or broaden changes without a verified rollback path.

data.name: Handoff_NextSteps
data.value: Review the HTML report, test cables and ports where relevant, check the device vendor's diagnostics, and create a privacy-redacted support package before requesting technical service.

data.name: Escalation_ModerateText
data.value: Safe steps were not enough. More comprehensive, reversible moderate repairs can be attempted.

data.name: Escalation_ForceText
data.value: This mode may reset drivers, network stacks, or system components. A full backup and System Restore point are mandatory.

data.name: Force_Accept
data.value: I understand the risks and authorize Force Repair.

data.name: LiveTests_Description
data.value: Measure connectivity, speakers, and microphone in real time. The speed test downloads data only when you start it.

data.name: LiveMetrics_Ping
data.value: Sent {0:0} Â· received {1:0} Â· loss {2:0.#}% Â· min/avg/max {3:0.0}/{4:0.0}/{5:0.0} ms Â· jitter {6:0.0} ms

data.name: LiveMetrics_Http
data.value: HTTP {0:0} Â· {1:0} ms Â· {2:0} bytes Â· expected page {3}

data.name: Recovery_Description
data.value: Roll back previous sessions in reverse order or inspect their backup folders.

data.name: Settings_DryRunDescription
data.value: Repairs are not applied; planned operations appear in the Operation Console.

data.name: Settings_ExpertDescription
data.value: Show a direct Safe, Moderate, and Force tier selector. All backup and consent gates remain enforced.

data.name: Settings_OfflineDescription
data.value: Local diagnostics and repairs work without internet access. Only internet, DNS, and speed tests require a connection.

data.name: Settings_LanguageDescription
data.value: English is the default. Turkish is used only when Windows is Turkish or you choose it here. Unsupported Windows languages fall back to English.

data.name: Dialog_LanguageRestart
data.value: CaYaFix must restart to apply the new language. Save any unfinished work, then continue.

data.name: Console_Hint
data.value: Commands and their output appear here live.

data.name: Status_Starting
data.value: Starting CaYaFix and checking recovery stateâ€¦

data.name: Dialog_ForceNeedsConsent
data.value: Accept the Force Repair risk confirmation before continuing.

data.name: Dialog_BackuplessAggressive
data.value: Force Repair may continue if an individual action backup cannot be captured. Automatic undo for that action will be unavailable. A System Restore point is still strongly recommended when available. Allow backup-less Aggressive actions in this run?

data.name: Dialog_SystemProtectionOff
data.value: Windows System Protection appears to be off or unavailable. CaYaFix will still try to create a restore point, and action-level backups remain mandatory for Safe and Moderate repairs. You can also skip this step with a warning.

data.name: Dialog_ExpertRunFix
data.value: Run â€œ{0}â€ ({1}) now? A backup is still required unless you later consent to backup-less Force Repair.

data.name: Verification_Preview
data.value: Preview finished for {0} selected finding(s). No changes were applied.

data.name: Verification_PendingReboot
data.value: Resolved so far: {0} Â· Waiting for reboot verification: {1}

data.name: Status_ExpertTargetMissing
data.value: Scan first so CaYaFix can collect a target for â€œ{0}â€.

data.name: Finding_Network_ThirdPartyLsp
data.value: Third-party Winsock LSP entries were detected and may intercept network traffic.

data.name: Finding_Network_WifiBand
data.value: Wiâ€‘Fi is stuck on a weak 2.4 GHz band or reports a low PHY rate.

data.name: Status_ModeChangedRescan
data.value: Preview mode changed. Run a new scan before applying repairs.

data.name: Status_NoSelection
data.value: Select at least one finding to repair.

data.name: Status_NoFixAtTier
data.value: No new repair is available at this tier.

data.name: Status_PreviewComplete
data.value: Preview complete for {0} selected finding(s). No changes were applied.

data.name: Status_PendingRebootSummary
data.value: The selected repair was applied and is waiting for one Windows restart before verification.

data.name: Dialog_RestorePointOffer
data.value:
CaYaFix can create a System Restore point before applying changes. This usually finishes within about 90 seconds, but Windows may show little progress while it works.
Yes = create restore point No = skip this step with a warning (action-level backups still run) Cancel = abort the repair

data.name: Dialog_SkipRestorePoint
data.value:
Continuing without a System Restore point means Windows cannot roll the whole system back via System Restore for this session. Safe and Moderate repairs still take action-level backups you can undo from Recovery.
Skip the restore point and continue?

data.name: Dialog_SkipRestoreAggressive
data.value:
Force Repair without a System Restore point is strongly discouraged. Action backups may also fail for some aggressive steps. Continue only if you accept that automatic recovery may be limited.
Skip the restore point and continue Force Repair?

data.name: Status_RollbackFailed
data.value: Some actions could not be rolled back. Check the Operation Console.

data.name: Dialog_RestorePointFailed_Title
data.value: Could not create a restore point

data.name: Dialog_RestorePointFailed
data.value: System Protection is disabled, the create step timed out (~90s), or Windows rejected the restore point. Action-level backups will still be required. Continue with safe or moderate repairs anyway?

data.name: Dialog_ForceNeedsRestore
data.value: A System Restore point was not created (disabled, timed out, or rejected). Force Repair is safer with System Restore available. Continue Force Repair without a restore point anyway?

data.name: Dialog_SupportPrivacy
data.value: The support package may include the computer name, network configuration, Wiâ€‘Fi information, and device or driver names. Share it only with people you trust. Create it now?

data.name: Dialog_Rollback
data.value: Applied actions will be restored in reverse order. Continue?

data.name: Dialog_Pending
data.value: A previous repair is waiting for a restart. Restart Windows, then open CaYaFix again to run its follow-up verification.

data.name: Dialog_Pending_Verify
data.value: Windows has restarted since the repair. Run the relevant diagnostic checks now to verify the result?

data.name: Dialog_InterruptedRepair
data.value: A repair did not reach a durable verification result. Its verified backup is available in Recovery Center. Roll it back before starting another repair.

data.name: Dialog_IntegrityFailure
data.value: CaYaFix could not initialize its protected session storage. No repairs were started. Check access to your local application-data folder, then start CaYaFix again.

data.name: Dialog_SingleInstanceFailure
data.value: CaYaFix could not acquire its system-wide operation lock. No repairs were started. Close other CaYaFix sessions and try again.

data.name: Dialog_DataRootFailure
data.value: CaYaFix cannot access its protected ProgramData folder. No diagnostics or repairs were started. Run the application as an administrator and check folder permissions.

data.name: Dialog_LiveTestConfirm
data.value: Did the test complete successfully from your perspective?

data.name: Status_PendingVerified
data.value: Restart verification passed. The pending repairs are complete.

data.name: Status_PendingStillDetected
data.value: Follow-up checks still detect an issue. Review the findings before trying another repair.

data.name: Status_PendingRolledBack
data.value: The post-restart diagnostic still detected the problem, so CaYaFix restored the verified pre-repair state.

data.name: Module_Network_Description
data.value: Wiâ€‘Fi, Ethernet, DNS, VPN, proxy, and firewall

data.name: Module_Audio_Description
data.value: Output, input, drivers, virtual devices, and Bluetooth audio

data.name: Module_Update_Description
data.value: Update services, cache, and error codes

data.name: Module_Printer_Description
data.value: Print queue, spooler, and offline printers

data.name: Module_Disk_Description
data.value: SMART health, free space, and high disk usage

data.name: Module_Integrity_Description
data.value: Windows component store, DISM, and SFC

data.name: Module_Store_Description
data.value: Microsoft Store and UWP package registrations

data.name: Module_Time_Description
data.value: NTP, clock drift, and hidden TLS failures

data.name: Module_Performance_Description
data.value: Startup items and abnormal power plans

data.name: Finding_Network_NoAdapters
data.value: Windows did not detect any network adapters.

data.name: Finding_Network_NoConnectedAdapter
data.value: No physical network adapter is currently connected. Connect Ethernet or Wi-Fi before running connection repairs.

data.name: Finding_Network_AllAdaptersDisabled
data.value: All physical network adapters are disabled or disconnected.

data.name: Finding_Network_OldDriver
data.value: A network driver is more than three years old.

data.name: Finding_Network_LinkAt100Mbps
data.value: The Ethernet link negotiated at 100 Mbps; the cable or port may be limiting speed.

data.name: Finding_Network_NoActiveIpConfiguration
data.value: No active IP configuration was found.

data.name: Finding_Network_Apipa
data.value: The computer could not obtain an address from the router and assigned itself a 169.254 address.

data.name: Finding_Network_MissingGateway
data.value: The connection has no default gateway.

data.name: Finding_Network_GatewayUnreachable
data.value: The computer cannot reach the modem or router.

data.name: Finding_Network_NoDnsServer
data.value: No usable DNS server is configured.

data.name: Finding_Network_CurrentDnsBroken
data.value: The current DNS server is not responding, while public DNS servers are working.

data.name: Finding_Network_InternetUnavailable
data.value: Windows could not reach the internet connectivity test.

data.name: Finding_Network_CaptivePortal
data.value: The connection may be redirecting to a sign-in or approval page.

data.name: Finding_Network_ProxyConfigured
data.value: A proxy or automatic configuration route is controlling internet traffic.

data.name: Finding_Network_VpnResidue
data.value: A disconnected VPN adapter left behind a route or DNS setting.

data.name: Finding_Network_FirewallBlocking
data.value: The firewall policy is broadly blocking outbound connections.

data.name: Finding_Network_FirewallDisabled
data.value: One or more Windows Firewall profiles are disabled.

data.name: Finding_Network_HostsMissing
data.value: The Windows hosts file is missing.

data.name: Finding_Network_CustomHosts
data.value: The hosts file contains custom entries that redirect internet addresses.

data.name: Finding_Network_WinsockUnreadable
data.value: The Winsock catalog could not be read.

data.name: Finding_Network_WinsockThirdParty
data.value: The Winsock catalog contains signs of a third-party network layer.

data.name: Finding_Network_MtuTooLow
data.value: An active connection has an unusually low MTU.

data.name: Finding_Network_PowerSavingEnabled
data.value: Windows is allowed to power down a network adapter.

data.name: Finding_Network_ServiceStopped
data.value: A required network service is not running correctly.

data.name: Finding_Network_TcpSettingsAbnormal
data.value: Windows TCP settings may be limiting connection speed.

data.name: Finding_Network_WeakWifi
data.value: The Wiâ€‘Fi signal is weak and may cause drops or reduced speed.

data.name: Finding_Network_ThroughputLow
data.value: Measured download throughput is unusually low.

data.name: Finding_Audio_NoOutputDevice
data.value: No active speaker or headset was found.

data.name: Finding_Audio_NoDefaultOutput
data.value: No default audio output is configured.

data.name: Finding_Audio_VirtualDefaultInactive
data.value: Audio is routed to an inactive virtual audio device.

data.name: Finding_Audio_DefaultUnavailable
data.value: The default audio device is currently unavailable.

data.name: Finding_Audio_ServiceStopped
data.value: A Windows audio service is stopped or disabled.

data.name: Finding_Audio_MutedOrZero
data.value: An audio device or application is muted or set to zero volume.

data.name: Finding_Audio_DriverError
data.value: An audio driver is reporting a Device Manager error code.

data.name: Finding_Audio_FormatMismatch
data.value: The input and output formats may be incompatible.

data.name: Finding_Audio_EnhancementCrash
data.value: A recent audio enhancement or APO failure was recorded.

data.name: Finding_Audio_MicrophonePrivacyDenied
data.value: Windows privacy settings are blocking microphone access.

data.name: Finding_Audio_BluetoothHandsFree
data.value: The Bluetooth headset is using the low-quality Hands-Free profile.

data.name: Finding_Audio_HdmiDefault
data.value: Audio may be routed to the monitor through HDMI or Display Audio.

data.name: Finding_Audio_DuckingInvalid
data.value: The communications volume-reduction setting is invalid.

data.name: Finding_Audio_SpeakerLiveFailed
data.value: The speaker test was not heard correctly.

data.name: Finding_Audio_MicrophoneLiveFailed
data.value: The microphone test did not produce a usable result.

data.name: Finding_Audio_StabilityLiveFailed
data.value: Audio playback stopped unexpectedly during the stability test.

data.name: Finding_Update_ServiceIssue
data.value: A Windows Update service is configured incorrectly.

data.name: Finding_Update_RecentErrors
data.value: Recent Windows Update errors were found.

data.name: Finding_Update_RebootPending
data.value: A Windows update is waiting for a restart.

data.name: Finding_Printer_SpoolerStopped
data.value: The Print Spooler service is not running.

data.name: Finding_Printer_QueueProblem
data.value: A print job is blocked or its queue is in an error state.

data.name: Finding_Printer_Offline
data.value: A configured printer reports that it is offline. Check its power, cable, Wi-Fi connection, and printer port.

data.name: Finding_Printer_DefaultMissing
data.value: Printers are installed, but Windows has no default printer.

data.name: Finding_Bluetooth_ServiceDisabled
data.value: The Bluetooth support service is disabled.

data.name: Finding_Bluetooth_DeviceError
data.value: A Bluetooth device is reporting a driver error code.

data.name: Finding_Disk_HealthCritical
data.value: A physical disk does not appear healthy. Back up important data before attempting repairs.

data.name: Finding_Disk_LowSpace
data.value: A drive is approaching critically low free space.

data.name: Finding_Disk_HighUsage
data.value: Disk utilization remained very high throughout the sample.

data.name: Finding_Integrity_Repairable
data.value: The Windows component store is repairable (DISM reported corruption that can be fixed online).

data.name: Finding_Integrity_NotRepairable
data.value: The Windows component store is corrupted and DISM reports it is not repairable online.

data.name: Finding_Integrity_DismFailed
data.value: DISM could not complete a reliable component-store health check.

data.name: Finding_Store_Missing
data.value: The Microsoft Store package is missing or not registered correctly.

data.name: Finding_Time_NotSynchronized
data.value: Windows is not synchronized with a time server.

data.name: Finding_Time_LocalClock
data.value: The system is relying only on the local hardware clock.

data.name: Finding_Performance_PowerSaver
data.value: The active Power Saver plan may be limiting performance.

data.name: FixResult_ApplyInProgress
data.value: The repair was interrupted before its result could be durably verified. Recovery is available.

data.name: FixResult_Verified
data.value: The repair was applied and verified by a fresh measurement.

data.name: FixResult_NotVerified
data.value: The repair was applied, but the issue is still present.

data.name: FixResult_PendingReboot
data.value: The repair was applied; verification requires a restart.

data.name: FixResult_BackupFailed
data.value: The repair was blocked because its backup failed.

data.name: FixResult_ApplyFailed
data.value: The repair could not be applied.

data.name: FixResult_ApplyFailedRolledBack
data.value: The repair failed, and all backed-up changes were automatically rolled back.

data.name: FixResult_ApplyFailedRollbackFailed
data.value: The repair failed, and automatic rollback could not be verified. Review the recovery session.

data.name: FixResult_VerificationFailedRolledBack
data.value: The repair did not pass verification, so its backed-up changes were automatically rolled back.

data.name: FixResult_VerificationFailedRollbackFailed
data.value: The repair did not pass verification, and automatic rollback could not be verified. Review the recovery session.

data.name: FixResult_CancelledRolledBack
data.value: The operation was cancelled and its backed-up changes were rolled back.

data.name: FixResult_CancelledRollbackFailed
data.value: The operation was cancelled, but automatic rollback could not be verified. Review the recovery session.

data.name: FixResult_CommandFailed
data.value: A repair step returned an error.

data.name: FixResult_DryRun
data.value: Preview mode: no change was applied.

data.name: FixResult_ForceRequirementsMissing
data.value: Force Repair requires explicit consent and a restore point.

data.name: FixResult_TargetRequired
data.value: This repair requires a target device.

data.name: TestStage_Recorded
data.value: The five-second microphone signal check completed

data.name: TestStage_CaptivePortal
data.value: Captive portal or unexpected response detected

data.name: Fix_Network_RenewDhcp
data.value: Renew DHCP on the active network profile

data.name: Fix_Network_RenewDhcpAll
data.value: Renew DHCP on all network adapters

data.name: Fix_Network_ProfileRepairActive
data.value: Repair the active network profile (DHCP, DNS, firewall)

data.name: Fix_Network_ProfileRepairAll
data.value: Repair all network profiles (DHCP, DNS, firewall)

data.name: Fix_Network_IpconfigSuite
data.value: Full ipconfig suite (flush / release / renew / register)

data.name: Fix_Network_ResetStack
data.value: Reset Winsock and the IP stack

data.name: Fix_Network_RemoveGhostVpn
data.value: Remove the selected ghost VPN adapter

data.name: Fix_Audio_RepairAllIo
data.value: Advanced repair: all audio input and output

data.name: Fix_Audio_BluetoothRestart
data.value: Restart the selected Bluetooth audio device

data.name: Fix_Audio_RescanDevices
data.value: Rescan audio devices and restart services

data.name: Fix_Printer_ResetQueue
data.value: Back up and clear the print queue

data.name: Fix_Disk_CleanTemp
data.value: Move old temporary files to quarantine

data.name: Fix_Integrity_RepairChain
data.value: Repair Windows with DISM and SFC

data.name: Fix_Performance_BalancedPlan
data.value: Switch to the Balanced power plan

data.name: Fix_Performance_RepairCoreServices
data.value: Repair core Windows services (start stopped / re-enable)

data.name: Finding_Performance_CoreServices
data.value: One or more core Windows services are disabled or stopped

data.name: Symptom_Performance_Services
data.value: Windows services feel broken or fail to start

data.name: Symptom_Network_Vpn
data.value: Internet broke after using a VPN

data.name: Symptom_Audio_WrongDevice
data.value: Sound comes from the wrong device

data.name: Symptom_Disk_Slow
data.value: Disk is slow or always busy

data.name: Symptom_Time_Wrong
data.value: Clock or time sync is wrong

data.name: Report_RollbackHelp
data.value: Open Recovery Center in CaYaFix and select this session. Actions are restored in reverse order.

data.name: Module_Camera_Description
data.value: Camera devices, access permissions, and recoverable device faults.

data.name: Module_Usb_Description
data.value: USB device errors and the Windows services behind device discovery.

data.name: Module_Search_Description
data.value: Search service health and recent indexing failures.

data.name: Module_Display_Description
data.value: Graphics adapters, resolution/mode health, monitors, and display-driver resets.

data.name: Finding_Camera_DeviceError
data.value: A camera device reports a Windows device error.

data.name: Finding_Camera_PrivacyBlocked
data.value: Windows privacy settings currently block camera access.

data.name: Finding_Usb_DeviceError
data.value: One or more connected USB devices report an error.

data.name: Finding_Usb_ServiceDisabled
data.value: A service required for device discovery is disabled.

data.name: Finding_Search_ServiceStopped
data.value: Windows Search is stopped or disabled.

data.name: Finding_Search_RecentErrors
data.value: Windows Search logged recent indexing errors.

data.name: Finding_Display_AdapterError
data.value: A graphics adapter reports a critical device error.

data.name: Finding_Display_DriverError
data.value: A graphics driver reports a Windows device error code.

data.name: Finding_Display_BasicAdapter
data.value: Windows is using the Microsoft Basic Display Adapter, which locks or greys out normal resolution choices.

data.name: Finding_Display_ModesLocked
data.value: Very few display modes are available â€” resolution may be stuck or greyed out even though the hardware can do more.

data.name: Finding_Display_SubNativeResolution
data.value: The current resolution is far below the highest mode the driver lists (single or multi-monitor).

data.name: Finding_Display_PolicyLocked
data.value: A policy appears to block changing display settings (resolution may be greyed out by design).

data.name: Finding_Display_MonitorError
data.value: A connected monitor device is disabled or in error, which can lock resolution options.

data.name: Finding_Display_GenericMonitors
data.value: Monitors are only identified as generic/unknown â€” EDID/mode lists may be incomplete.

data.name: Finding_Display_NoMonitor
data.value: No present monitor devices were found for mode enumeration.

data.name: Finding_Display_RecentResets
data.value: The display driver was reset recently.

data.name: Finding_Display_TdrEvents
data.value: Windows logged recent GPU timeout (TDR) or display recovery events.

data.name: Finding_Network_EventLogErrors
data.value: Recent Windows event log errors were correlated for network adapters or TCP/IP.

data.name: Finding_Network_ProtocolDisabled
data.value: IPv4 or IPv6 is disabled on one or more physical adapters.

data.name: Finding_Audio_EventLogErrors
data.value: Recent Windows event log errors were correlated for audio services.

data.name: Finding_Audio_PnpDisabled
data.value: One or more audio devices are disabled or in an error state in Device Manager.

data.name: Fix_Display_RepairResolution
data.value: Repair stuck resolution (adapters, monitors, soft-reset, rescan)

data.name: Fix_Display_RestoreRecommended
data.value: Apply highest supported resolution on each display

data.name: Finding_Integrity_ScanHealthFailed
data.value: DISM ScanHealth found component-store corruption that should be repaired (not a clean scan).

data.name: Symptom_Usb_NotWorking
data.value: A USB device is not working

data.name: Symptom_Display_ResolutionStuck
data.value: Cannot change resolution (greyed out / stuck) though the screen supports more

data.name: TestResult_NoActiveAdapter
data.value: No active IPv4 adapter is available for this test.

data.name: TestResult_IncompleteDownload
data.value: The download ended early at {0:0.0} MB.

data.name: TestResult_MtuUnavailable
data.value: {0}: the ICMP path MTU probe is unavailable

data.name: LiveMetrics_Mtu
data.value: Path MTU: {0:0} bytes Â· ICMP payload: {1:0} bytes

data.name: UpdateError_80070002
data.value: The update file could not be found.

data.name: UpdateError_8007000E
data.value: There is not enough memory to complete the operation.

data.name: UpdateError_80244022
data.value: The update service is temporarily unavailable.

data.name: UpdateError_800F081F
data.value: A required component-store source could not be found.

data.name: Dialog_AlreadyRunning
data.value: CaYaFix is already running for this Windows user.

data.name: Dialog_RollbackAction
data.value: The selected action will be restored from its verified backup. Continue?

data.name: Dialog_CancelRollback
data.value: Stopping now will leave any already restored actions as they are. Remaining actions will not be rolled back. Stop the rollback?

data.name: Dialog_RollbackCancelled
data.value: The rollback was cancelled. Actions already restored remain restored. Review Recovery Center before starting new repairs.

data.name: Status_RollbackCancelled
data.value: Rollback cancelled. Review Recovery Center for remaining actions.

data.name: Dialog_DismissRecoveryAction
data.value: This marks the interrupted or failed recovery item as acknowledged without restoring the backup. Only continue if you understand the system may still be partially changed.

data.name: Dialog_DismissRecoverySession
data.value: All interrupted or failed recovery items in this session will be dismissed without rolling them back. Continue only if you accept leaving those changes as-is.

data.name: Status_RecoveryDismissFailed
data.value: The recovery item could not be dismissed.

data.name: FixResult_RecoveryDismissed
data.value: Recovery was dismissed by the user without automatic rollback.

data.name: Operation_PreparingRestorePoint
data.value: Creating a System Restore point before changesâ€¦

data.name: Operation_RestorePointChecking
data.value: Checking System Protection and recent restore pointsâ€¦

data.name: Operation_RestorePointWorking
data.value: Windows is creating the restore point (up to ~90 seconds)â€¦

data.name: Operation_RestorePointWaiting
data.value: Still creating the restore pointâ€¦ {0}s elapsed. You can skip with a warning.

data.name: Operation_RestorePointAlreadyPresent
data.value: A recent System Restore point is already available â€” create skipped.

data.name: Operation_RestorePointSkipped
data.value: System Restore point skipped with user warning. Action-level backups still apply.

data.name: Operation_RestorePointFailedDetail
data.value: Restore point step did not succeed: {0}

data.name: ModulePanel_Symptoms
data.value: Common symptoms â€” pick one to run a focused scan

data.name: ModulePanel_NoSymptoms
data.value: No symptom shortcuts for this area. Run a full module scan instead.

data.name: ModulePanel_Hint
data.value: Scan this area for deeper checks, or pick a symptom to apply related repairs after a risk warning (side effects such as crackling, disconnects, or flicker may appear while repairs run).

data.name: Operation_LiveSummary
data.value: Healthy checks: {0} Â· Findings so far: {1}

data.name: Operation_ResultHealthyDetail
data.value: {0} checks completed cleanly. Nothing looks broken right now.

data.name: Operation_ResultHealthyAdvice
data.value: You can still open live tests if something feels intermittent.

data.name: Operation_ResultAdvisoryDetail
data.value: {0} finding(s) were recorded, and {1} look like common/advisory situations (for example virtual audio devices, weak Wiâ€‘Fi, or power-saving settings).

data.name: Operation_ResultAdvisoryAdvice
data.value: These often appear on machines with Voicemeeter, cable drivers, docking stations, or VPN software and are not always a real fault. Review them before forcing repairs.

data.name: Operation_ResultMixedDetail
data.value: {0} likely real issue(s), {1} common/advisory finding(s), and {2} clean checks.

data.name: Operation_ResultMixedAdvice
data.value: Start with â€œFix real issuesâ€. Common findings such as virtual drivers can be normal and may be misleading if repaired first.

data.name: Operation_ResultIssuesAdvice
data.value: Use â€œFix real issuesâ€ to start Safe repairs for selected findings. You can still open the findings list for full control.

data.name: Finding_Network_PersistentRouteConflict
data.value: Conflicting or unreachable persistent network routes were detected.

data.name: Finding_Disk_FileSystemDirty
data.value: Windows marked the system volume for a file-system consistency check.

data.name: Settings_ExpertCatalogDescription
data.value: Full repair catalog. Use Run to apply a single repair (backup and consent gates still apply). Target-required repairs need a prior scan that captured the target.

data.name: Settings_ManualToolsDescription
data.value: Pick a Microsoft-oriented repair tool and run it without waiting for a scan finding. Safe and Moderate tools are always available; Aggressive tools need Force risk acceptance first.

data.name: Settings_ManualToolsRisk
data.value: These tools change system state (ipconfig, DISM/SFC, WinRE/BCD, network stack, GPU soft-reset, and more). Backups and confirmations still apply. Boot rebuild can require a restart; offline-only commands such as bootrec stay in Windows Recovery Environment.

data.name: Settings_ManualToolsAggressiveHint
data.value: Accept Force Repair risk on Findings, then run the Aggressive tool again.

data.name: Module_Boot_Description
data.value: WinRE, BCD health, and online boot-file recovery (bcdedit / bcdboot / reagentc).

data.name: Finding_Boot_WinReUnreadable
data.value: WinRE status could not be read with reagentc.

data.name: Finding_Boot_BcdUnreadable
data.value: The current Boot Configuration Data entry could not be read.

data.name: Finding_Boot_FirmwareEnumFailed
data.value: Firmware BCD enumeration failed while the OS loader entry is also unhealthy.

data.name: Fix_Boot_RecoveryEnabled
data.value: Enable recovery on current boot entry

data.name: Finding_Disk_OnlineScanIssues
data.value: Online file-system scan reported issues or did not complete cleanly.

data.name: Finding_Integrity_CleanupRecommended
data.value: DISM recommends WinSxS component-store cleanup (space reclaim â€” not file corruption).

data.name: Symptom_Integrity_Cleanup
data.value: Windows component store is large / cleanup recommended

data.name: GuidedRepair_Effects_Boot
data.value: Boot/recovery tools may export BCD, enable WinRE, or rewrite boot files. A restart can be required. Offline-only tools (bootrec) are not run from a running desktop session.

data.name: Finding_Store_PackageUnhealthy
data.value: The Microsoft Store package reports an unhealthy state.

data.name: Finding_Store_RecentErrors
data.value: Microsoft Store logged recent operational errors.

data.name: Dialog_CloseBusy
data.value: CaYaFix must cancel the active operation, finish any required rollback, and save the session before it can close. Continue?

data.name: TestStage_PlaybackUnavailable
data.value: The microphone signal was detected, but playback was unavailable

data.name: Fix_Usb_CycleRootHubs
data.value: Restart USB root hubs and rescan

data.name: Fix_Search_SetAutomatic
data.value: Set Windows Search to Automatic and start it

data.name: Fix_Search_RebuildIndex
data.value: Rebuild the Windows Search index (requires restart)

data.name: Fix_Search_ClearTemp
data.value: Clear Windows Search temporary indexer data

data.name: Fix_Search_RestartAndRebuild
data.value: Restart Search and rebuild the index

data.name: Fix_Store_ClearLocalCache
data.value: Clear Microsoft Store local package cache

data.name: Fix_Store_ResetAppxPackage
data.value: Reset the Microsoft Store app package

data.name: Fix_Time_ResyncRediscover
data.value: Resync time and rediscover NTP peers

data.name: Fix_Time_SetManualNtp
data.value: Set time.windows.com as manual NTP peer

data.name: Fix_Time_ConfigUpdate
data.value: Apply Windows Time config update and resync

data.name: Fix_Update_ScDefaults
data.value: Restore default Windows Update service start modes

data.name: Fix_Disk_CleanUserTemp
data.value: Clean old files in the user temp folder

data.name: Fix_Disk_FlushVolume
data.value: Flush the system volume write cache

data.name: Fix_Disk_ClearVolumeHints
data.value: Query volume free space and dirty bit

data.name: Fix_Printer_PurgeSpool
data.value: Purge the print spooler queue folder

data.name: Fix_Printer_ScAutoSpooler
data.value: Set Print Spooler to Automatic and start it

data.name: Fix_Printer_EnsureSpoolFolder
data.value: Ensure the spool PRINTERS folder exists

data.name: Fix_Boot_BootStatusPolicyIgnore
data.value: Set boot status policy to IgnoreAllFailures

data.name: Fix_Boot_BootStatusPolicyDisplay
data.value: Set boot status policy to DisplayAllFailures

data.name: Fix_Boot_DisableRecoveryEnabled
data.value: Disable recoveryenabled for current boot entry

data.name: Fix_Performance_HighPerformancePlan
data.value: Switch to the High performance power plan

data.name: Fix_Performance_QueryEnergy
data.value: Run a short powercfg energy report

data.name: Symptom_Search_SlowIndex
data.value: Search index is slow or never finishes

data.name: Symptom_Store_InstallFail
data.value: Microsoft Store apps fail to install or update

data.name: Symptom_Time_NoSource
data.value: No valid time source / NTP peer

data.name: Module_Defender_Description
data.value: Defender antivirus, real-time protection, signature updates, firewall profiles, and Security Center health.

data.name: Module_Shell_Description
data.value: File Explorer stability, icon and thumbnail caches, font cache, user folders, jump lists, and folder views.

data.name: Module_Policy_Description
data.value: Unblocks Task Manager, Registry Editor, Command Prompt, Control Panel, and Windows Update policy locks; refreshes or resets local Group Policy.

data.name: Module_Wmi_Description
data.value: WMI service and repository consistency, Windows Event Log health, and performance counter repair.

data.name: Finding_Defender_ServicesUnhealthy
data.value: Windows Security services are disabled or stopped.

data.name: Finding_Defender_RealtimeOff
data.value: Defender real-time protection is off and no other antivirus is active.

data.name: Finding_Defender_FirewallOff
data.value: One or more firewall profiles are disabled.

data.name: Finding_Defender_PolicyBlocked
data.value: A policy value is blocking Windows Defender.

data.name: Finding_Shell_ExplorerNotRunning
data.value: The Explorer shell is not running.

data.name: Finding_Shell_CacheHeavy
data.value: Icon/thumbnail caches are unusually large and may be corrupted.

data.name: Finding_Shell_UserFolderMissing
data.value: A mapped user folder does not exist on disk.

data.name: Finding_Shell_ServicesUnhealthy
data.value: Shell support services are disabled or stopped.

data.name: Finding_Policy_TaskManagerBlocked
data.value: Task Manager is blocked by a restriction policy.

data.name: Finding_Policy_RegistryBlocked
data.value: Registry Editor is blocked by a restriction policy.

data.name: Finding_Policy_CmdBlocked
data.value: Command Prompt is blocked by a restriction policy.

data.name: Finding_Policy_ControlPanelBlocked
data.value: Control Panel and Settings are blocked by a restriction policy.

data.name: Finding_Policy_UpdateLocked
data.value: Windows Update is limited by policy values.

data.name: Finding_Wmi_ServiceIssue
data.value: The WMI service is not healthy.

data.name: Finding_Wmi_EventLogIssue
data.value: The Windows Event Log service or a log is unhealthy.

data.name: Finding_Wmi_PerfCountersBroken
data.value: Performance counters could not be read.

data.name: Fix_Policy_ResetLocalGpo
data.value: Reset local Group Policy to defaults

data.name: Symptom_Defender_Off
data.value: Windows Security is off or will not open

data.name: Symptom_Defender_Firewall
data.value: Firewall is off or blocking connections

data.name: Symptom_Shell_Unstable
data.value: Explorer crashes; taskbar or Start misbehaves

data.name: Symptom_Shell_IconsBroken
data.value: Icons, thumbnails, or fonts look broken

data.name: Symptom_Policy_Blocked
data.value: Task Manager, Regedit, or CMD is blocked

data.name: Symptom_Wmi_Broken
data.value: WMI errors, broken counters, or Event Log issues

data.name: GuidedRepair_Effects_Defender
data.value: Security services restart briefly; signature updates need internet and a quick scan can take several minutes.

data.name: GuidedRepair_Effects_Shell
data.value: The taskbar and desktop reload briefly and open Explorer windows close; icon and thumbnail caches rebuild over time.

data.name: GuidedRepair_Effects_Policy
data.value: Restriction policy values are removed and Group Policy is refreshed; devices managed by an organization may re-apply settings.

data.name: GuidedRepair_Effects_Wmi
data.value: Management services restart; monitoring tools and dependent services may reconnect after a short delay.
